persona_json.name: Garrett, the Infrastructure-First Platform Engineer
persona_json.summary: Garrett is a Staff Engineer or Senior DevOps Engineer at a mid-sized SaaS or FinTech company (50-500 employees) who builds and maintains production systems integrating external APIs. He prioritizes infrastructure-as-code, CI/CD automation, and reliable bulk data operations, and actively seeks API platforms that can scale without rough edges or vendor lock-in concerns.

demographics.education_level: Bachelor's in Computer Science or related field

goals: Set up reliable API integrations with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform)
Automate data export workflows to handle 500k+ row bulk operations without timeouts
Integrate APIs seamlessly into CI/CD pipelines for production reliability
Configure webhooks and custom dashboards for real-time monitoring
Scale API usage in production without hitting undocumented limits or performance cliffs
Reduce manual integration work through SDKs and GitHub-native tooling
pains: GraphQL endpoints with incomplete documentation or inconsistent behavior compared to REST
Bulk export operations timing out or failing when handling large datasets (500k rows)
Unclear API limits or scaling behavior in high-volume production scenarios
Integration complexity requiring multiple tools and manual configuration steps
Lack of clear guidance on best practices for scaling API usage in production environments
Missing or outdated SDK examples for infrastructure-as-code patterns
Difficulty monitoring API performance across distributed systems
motivations: Desire to build production-grade systems that are reliable and scalable
Need to automate repetitive infrastructure and integration tasks
Drive to use industry-standard tools (Terraform, GitHub, CI/CD) without workarounds
Preference for APIs that 'just work' without surprises in production
Want to minimize operational overhead and maintenance burden
Commitment to infrastructure transparency and vendor-neutral architecture
objections: GraphQL implementation feels unfinished or poorly designed compared to REST alternative
Performance concerns with bulk operations that don't scale reliably
Unclear pricing or limits for high-volume API usage
Concerned about vendor lock-in with proprietary SDK or integration patterns
Lack of best-practice documentation for production deployments
Unwilling to adopt APIs that require heavy custom code or workarounds
channels: GitHub (SDK repos, community discussions, issue tracking)
Direct engineering teams and peer recommendations
decision_triggers: Clear documentation showing the API handles 500k+ row exports without timing out
Evidence that GraphQL is production-ready and on feature parity with REST
Concrete best-practice guides for scaling API usage across multiple environments
Native Terraform integration or well-maintained SDKs that follow industry standards
Transparent pricing and documented limits for high-volume API usage
Success stories from similar SaaS/FinTech companies using the API at scale
sample_quotes: Your REST API is solid but the GraphQL endpoint has some rough edges.
We need the bulk export to handle 500k rows without timing out.
I want to manage this API integration through Terraform just like everything else in our infrastructure.
Show me concrete limits and scaling guidance before we bet our production system on this.

journey_stages.mindset: Evaluating whether an API platform can meet our production reliability and scale requirements
key_actions: Exploring API documentation and reference pages
Testing SDK installation and basic integration
Reviewing GitHub integration and CI/CD capabilities
content_preferences: Integration examples with Terraform and GitHub Actions

journey_stages.mindset: Determining if the platform's GraphQL and bulk export features are reliable enough for production and whether it aligns with our infrastructure-as-code practices
key_actions: Setting up webhook configurations for monitoring
Attempting bulk export with realistic data volumes
Engaging with support on scaling concerns
content_preferences: Technical deep-dives on GraphQL vs. REST capabilities

journey_stages.mindset: Ready to commit if the platform clearly addresses high-volume data handling, provides transparent scaling guidance, and avoids vendor lock-in
key_actions: Requesting performance testing for 500k+ row exports
Negotiating SLAs and support for high-volume usage
Finalizing SDK and CI/CD integration strategy
content_preferences: Direct technical review and engineering pairing sessions
